title: syntax-rules: quote/quasiquote SHORTHAND as a whole rule template is rejected ('expected closing paren after macro rule template')
goal: Found by the P6e metaprogramming depth harness (2026-07-03). A syntax-rules rule whose ENTIRE template is a reader-macro shorthand form -- quote 'x or quasiquote `x -- fails to parse with 'expected closing paren after macro rule template'. The long form (quote ...) works, and the shorthand works when NESTED inside a larger template (e.g. (cons x '())), so the bug is specifically the template reader not accepting a top-level ' or ` shorthand. This blocks the idiomatic recursive-macro base case ((_) '()). Workaround in the harness: use (quote ()) long form.
acceptance: (define-syntax q (syntax-rules () ((_ x) `(a ,x)))) parses (see ESH-0119 for the substitution bug)
harness recmac_list base case can use '() instead of (quote ())
campaign_payoff: Recursive macros with a quoted base case are textbook R7RS; rejecting the shorthand breaks common macro code.
repro: printf "(define-syntax nil (syntax-rules () ((_) '())))\n(display (nil))(newline)\n" > /tmp/t.esk; build/eshkol-run -r /tmp/t.esk  # error: expected closing paren after macro rule template
